# End-to-end: add jobs, run the daemon, verify they fire, stop.
# This is the closest thing the test suite has to a production-shaped
# integration scenario — it goes through the binary, the scheduler, and
# SQLite together, and asserts on observable behaviour only.

# Add a cron that ticks every second and a one-shot 2s out.
exec eon add --cron '@every 1s' --name ticker -- echo tick
exec eon add --at '+2s' --name once -- echo oneshot
exec eon ls
stdout 'ticker'
stdout 'once'

# Launch the daemon in the background.
exec eon daemon &daemon&

# Give it time to claim the slot. Polling would be tighter, but a
# fixed sleep is enough for the slowest CI host.
exec sleep 2
exec eon status
stdout 'daemon:     running'

# Let cron fire ~3 times and the oneshot fire exactly once.
exec sleep 4

exec eon show ticker --json
stdout '"last_status": "ok"'
stdout '"status": "enabled"'

exec eon show once --json
stdout '"status": "done"'
stdout '"last_status": "ok"'

# Cron logs should contain the tick.
exec eon logs ticker
stdout 'tick'

# Oneshot logs.
exec eon logs once
stdout 'oneshot'

# Bring it down.
exec eon stop
stdout 'daemon stopped'
wait daemon

# After stop the slot is clear.
exec eon status
stdout 'daemon:     stopped'
